untranslatable_reason: This signal requires options pricing data (straddle credit/debit spreads and strike price proximity) which are not available in the allowed indicators list. The strategy involves comparing near-term vs longer-term straddle option premiums with a specific ratio constraint (credit >= 2/3 of debit), which cannot be expressed using price action, moving averages, RSI, ATR, Bollinger Bands, MACD, ADX, Stochastic, Donchian, pivots, or volume indicators.
translation_notes: MCMILLAN_DRY_151 is a calendar spread options strategy that fundamentally requires: (1) options chain data to calculate straddle premiums, (2) the ability to measure distance to strike price, and (3) time-to-expiration calculations for both near-term and longer-term contracts. None of these data sources are available in the equity/technical indicator universe. This strategy cannot be backtested using only price and technical indicators.
